executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict intensifies into day 4 with major new developments: IRGC claims a US tanker is on fire in the Strait of Hormuz, signaling direct maritime escalation; Israel has flown over 1,000 combat sorties inside Iran; and Qatar's air defense interceptors face depletion within 4 days. Bahrain has now intercepted 70 missiles and 59 drones. Gulf oil/gas production remains shut. The conflict has expanded geographically to include British territory in Cyprus and Pakistan (23 protesters killed over Khamenei's death).
whats_new: IRGC claims US tanker on fire in Strait of Hormuz after drone strike — major maritime escalation
Qatar interceptor stock will exhaust in 4 days at current usage rate — critical air defense sustainability issue
Iran launched attacks on British base in Cyprus — conflict expands to European sovereign territory
Kuwait announces first military death in conflict — escalation of regional involvement
US B-1 bombers conducted strikes deep inside Iranian territory targeting ballistic missile capabilities
Israeli Air Force conducted over 1,000 combat flights over Iranian territory — sustained deep-strike campaign
23 protesters killed in Pakistan after killing of Khamenei — regional instability spreading
confirmed_events: UAE air defenses successfully intercepted 9 ballistic missiles, 6 cruise missiles, and 148 drones — confirmed by UAE Ministry of Defense
Qatar shot down two Iranian Su-24 combat aircraft entering Qatari airspace — confirmed by Qatar Ministry of Defense
Bahrain Defense Force intercepted new wave of Iranian missiles/drones; total 70 missiles and 59 drones intercepted to date
Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon killed 52 and wounded 154 — confirmed by Lebanese Health Ministry
Iranian IRGC and Army launched fresh waves of missiles/drones against US and Israeli targets — confirmed by IRGC statements
unverified_emerging: Explosions reported near Isfahan with strikes targeting nuclear and air defense facilities — source unconfirmed but attributed to Israel/US (single source: social media reports)
Iranian provincial official claims approximately 100 fishing boats caught fire following attack in southern Iran — details unverified (single source: Press TV)
Unverified social media claim of Iranian total naval and air blockade on Gulf countries including Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, UAE, and partially Saudi Arabia
Iranian Armed Forces claim to have shot down seven US and Israeli drones in recent hours — unverified casualty/confirmation data
US-Israeli strike on Shajrat Taybeh girls' elementary school in Minab killed 165 civilians including students — requires independent verification

people_travel: US State Department orders all personnel in Israel, West Bank, Gaza to continue sheltering in place
Airspace closures in UAE and Gulf states continue; tourists stranded in Dubai
Kuwait airspace impacted by ongoing strikes — pilot ejection incident reported
supply_chain: Gulf oil and gas production remains shut down — significant global supply impact
Strait of Hormuz maritime traffic disrupted by tanker attack — potential shipping lane bottleneck
Qatar air defense interceptor depletion in 4 days threatens sustained defensive capability
market_macro: Gulf oil production shutdown driving global energy price volatility
Prediction markets active on Iran conflict outcomes — including bets on Khamenei death
Regional stock markets face significant uncertainty amid conflict escalation

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict enters decisive phase with Iran launching 12+ waves of attacks and Israel conducting 1,000+ sorties inside Iran. US expanding troop deployments. Qatar's interceptor depletion in 4 days creates urgent sustainability question for Gulf air defense. Iranian retaliation capability remains substantial but degrading under sustained US/Israeli strikes. Gulf states increasingly pressuring for diplomatic solution while actively engaging Iranian threats. Risk of Strait of Hormuz maritime incident causing global energy crisis is HIGH.
escalation_risks: Strait of Hormuz tanker attack could trigger global oil supply shock if sustained
Qatar interceptor depletion could collapse air defense over US Al Udeid base within 96 hours
Conflict expansion to Cyprus brings NATO territory into direct Iranian targeting
Iranian proxies (Hezbollah, Houthis) could open additional fronts
Pakistan instability following Khamenei death could draw additional actors into conflict
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: UAE and Qatar actively pressuring Trump to end bombing campaign — diplomatic channel exists but currently marginal. Iranian leadership vacuum after Khamenei's death creates potential negotiation window but IRGC hardliners currently control response. Russian statements supporting Bahrain's sovereignty indicate Moscow may play mediator role. China's diplomatic engagement with Iran offers back-channel. However, stated US objective of 'regime defeat' and Iranian IRGC commitment to 'keep doors of fire open' suggest both sides currently positioned for continued hostilities rather than compromise.
